contract.name: v2/disaster/recipient/spending.md
contract.description: Aggregates disaster and emergency supplemental spending by recipient with optional sorting and filters.

filter.description: Primary filter object. The only mandatory key is def_codes; other shared filters are accepted but may have no effect if the dataset lacks matching records.

filter.def_codes.description: List of Disaster Emergency Fund (DEFC) codes to aggregate over.
filter.def_codes.constraints: required; minItems=1; accepts uppercase codes from the enumerated list (digits 1-9, single letters A-Z, multi-letter codes such as AAA-AAL and QQQ); must be sent as an array; empty array -> 422; invalid code -> 400 with allowed list

filter.award_type_codes.description: Optional award type filter limiting the aggregated recipients.
filter.award_type_codes.constraints: optional; minItems=1; allowed values ['-1','02','03','04','05','06','07','08','09','10','11','A','B','C','D','IDV_A','IDV_B','IDV_B_A','IDV_B_B','IDV_B_C','IDV_C','IDV_D','IDV_E']; empty array -> 422; other codes -> 400

filter.query.description: Keyword search applied to recipient text fields.
filter.query.constraints: optional; must be non-empty; empty string -> 422; unmatched terms return 200 with zero totals

filter.time_period.description: Accepted list of Search v2 time_period objects (start_date/end_date/date_type).
filter.time_period.constraints: optional; payloads are accepted but observed responses ignore the supplied bounds

pagination.constraints: optional; defaults to page=1, limit=10, order='desc', sort='id' (which immediately falls back to description and emits a warning)

pagination.limit.description: Number of recipient rows per page.
pagination.limit.constraints: optional; accepts integers 1-100 inclusive; numeric strings are coerced; floats trigger 400; values outside range trigger 422

pagination.page.constraints: optional; minimum 1; zero or negative values return 422; high pages return empty results with metadata

pagination.sort.constraints: optional; allowed values ['award_count','description','code','id','obligation','outlay']; default 'id' triggers a warning and sorts by description

pagination.order.description: Sort direction for the selected field.
pagination.order.constraints: optional; allowed values ['asc','desc']; invalid strings return 400

spending_type.description: Determines which metric drives the ranking and totals.
spending_type.constraints: optional; observed values ['total','obligation','outlay','award_count']; null or unknown strings silently revert to 'total'

totals.description: Aggregate sums across all recipients that match the filters.

obligation.description: Total obligations for matching recipients (can be negative).

outlay.description: Total outlays for matching recipients (can be negative).

award_count.description: Number of awards included in the aggregate.

results.description: Recipient rollups for the requested page; may be empty when no records match.

id.description: Array of award identifiers backing the row; null for aggregated pseudo-recipients in award_count mode.

code.description: Recipient identifier (UEI/DUNS or synthetic bucket code).

award_count.description: Number of awards included in this recipient grouping.

obligation.description: Total obligations attributed to the recipient in the selected view.

outlay.description: Total outlays attributed to the recipient in the selected view.

page_metadata.description: Pagination metadata for the current request.

total.description: Total number of recipient rows available for the query.

limit.description: Page size used in this response.

next.description: Next page number when additional results exist, otherwise null.

previous.description: Previous page number when available, otherwise null.

hasNext.description: True when more results follow the current page.

messages.description: Optional warning strings (commonly surfaced when sort='id' falls back to description).

messages: Notice! API Request to sort on 'id' field isn't fully implemented. Results were actually sorted using 'description' field.

quirks: Default pagination.sort of 'id' falls back to description ordering and emits a warning message.
Result id values become null for aggregated pseudo-recipients when spending_type='award_count'.
time_period filters are accepted but ignored in observed responses.
Invalid or null spending_type values silently revert to 'total'.
risks: Clients that rely on default sorting must handle warning messages and implicit description ordering.
Downstream consumers must tolerate null result ids in award_count mode.
Date-constrained analyses can be misleading because time_period filters do not affect the dataset.
gaps: Unknown which recipient fields the query filter searches across.
Unclear whether additional shared filters (e.g., recipient_type_names, agencies) have any effect beyond being syntactically accepted.
Possible additional spending_type values beyond the four observed remain undocumented.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"totals": {"obligation": 4291442486.72, "outlay": 6192693427.16, "award_count": 5403}, "results": [{"id": ["fff8e22a-ecad-1885-d9a5-ae95e8c6fa27-C"...}] , "messages": ["Notice! API Request to sort on 'id' field isn't fully implemented. Results were actually sorted using 'description' field."]}

probes.notes: Baseline request succeeds but triggers the id-sort fallback warning.
Explicit sort=description removes the warning and enforces alphabetical ordering.
Award_count mode returns aggregated rows with id=null, contradicting doc schema.
Invalid sort values are rejected with a 400 error listing the accepted options.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"totals":{"obligation":0.0,"outlay":0.0,"award_count":0},"results":[],"page_metadata":{"total":0,...},"messages":["Notice! API Request to sort on 'id' field isn't fully implemented..."]}

probes.notes: Unmatched keywords return an empty result set but still emit the id-sort warning when sort is not overridden.

mismatches: Docs require Result.id to be an array of strings, but award_count responses return null for aggregated recipients.
Docs list pagination.sort options as description/award_count/obligation/outlay, yet the API also accepts code and id (id emits a warning and sorts by description).
Docs do not mention the spending_type parameter even though the endpoint accepts 'total', 'obligation', 'outlay', and 'award_count'.
Shared filter docs imply time_period constrains results, but observed responses ignore the supplied ranges.
gaps: Unknown which recipient fields the query filter searches across.
Unclear whether additional shared filters (e.g., recipient_type_names, agencies) have any effect beyond being syntactically accepted.
Possible additional spending_type values beyond the four observed remain undocumented.
risks: Clients that rely on default sorting must handle warning messages and implicit description ordering.
Downstream consumers must tolerate null result ids in award_count mode.
Date-constrained analyses can be misleading because time_period filters do not affect the dataset.
